title: Phagocytosis Bead Conjugation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7r30n","text":"Protocols for the amine coupling of fluorescent microspheres with antigen or capture reagent for opsonization and phagocytosis","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aeaas","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6mri1","text":"Quantity as written: approx. 1 x 108 under typical yields (enough for 400 phagocytosis wells under standard protocol)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":35,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Resuspend beads by briefly vortexing; dispense 200 µL, (contains appox. 7 billion beads)
Pellet by centrifugation 00:03:00 and remove supernatant by pipette

Resuspend in a total of 1 mL consisting of: - sulfo-NHS 50 mg/mL made in 10 millimolar (mM) MES (pH 5.0) 100 µL - EDC 50 mg/mL , made in 10 millimolar (mM) MES (pH 5.0) 100 µL - 10 millimolar (mM) MES (pH 5.0) 800 µL
Incubate with end-over-end mixing (shielded from light) 00:20:00 Room temperature
Wash by pelleting beads and replacing supernatant with 10 millimolar (mM) MES (pH 5.0) 1 mL 00:02:00

Formulate protein for coupling @ approx. 25 µg/mL in 10 millimolar (mM) sodium acetate (pH 5.0) 7.5 mL n.b. The large volume used for the step is based on observations that sufficiently high bead concentrations during this step may lead to crosslinking between microspheres ( anecdotal )
Incubate with end-over-end mixing (shielded from light) 03:00:00 Room temperature
Split the 7.5 mL volume into multiple fractions in microcentrifuge tubes ( 2 mL ) in order to centrifuge at a sufficiently high speed 00:03:00
go to step #11 for a total of 3 washes
Resuspend in PBSF 500 µL
Collect the full volume in the base of the reaction tube by centrifugation 00:01:00
Remove supernatant from each fraction by pipette
Resuspend one fraction in PBSF (ie. 1x PBS + 0.1 Mass / % volume BSA) 1 mL
Transfer the entire contents of the first fraction to the second and resuspend; continue through the fractions until as many beads as possible have been collected in a single microcentrifuge tube
Pellet beads via centrifuge 00:02:00
Block via 16:00:00, (ie. overnight) 4 °C
Make serial dilutions of 1:100, 1:1000, and 1:10,000

Measure concentration by flow cytometry using the fluorescent dye of the microsphere as the trigger condition Typical yield is approx. 1 - 2 x 10 8 beads, or around 25% of the original amount MACS Quant (flow cytometer, Miltenyi, 130-096-343)
In a non-binding, 96-well plate, formulate serial dilutions of positive and negative control antibodies beginning at 5 µg/mL and diluted by 1:3 over an approx. 4 point series
Add 100,000 beads to each well
Incubate with shaking (shielded from light) 01:00:00 Room temperature
Pellet by centrifugation 00:05:00 and remove supernatant by decanting

Resuspend beads in fluorescent secondary 100 µL 0.65 µg/mL n.b. Make sure that the fluorescent molecule chosen for the secondary does not have spectral overlap with the dye in the microspheres themselves
Resuspend beads in PBST (ie. 1x PBS + 0.05 % volume Tween20) 200 µL
go to step #24 for a total of 2 washes
Pellet by centrifugation 00:05:00 and remove supernatant by decanting
Resuspend beads in PBST (ie. 1x PBS + 0.05 % volume Tween20) 200 µL
Incubate with shaking (shielded from light) 00:30:00 Room temperature
Pellet by centrifugation 00:05:00 and remove supernatant by decanting
Resuspend beads in 1x PBS 100 µL
Measure fluorescent intensity by flow cytometry MACS Quant (flow cytometer, Miltenyi, 130-096-343)
Calculate the beads / mL from the percent solids as follows: (6W x 10 12 ) / (ρ x π x φ 3 ) W = grams solid per mL ρ = density (g/mL) φ = particle diameter (µm) polystyrene density = 1.055 /mL

Collect beads by pulse centrifugation 00:00:03 and place in magnetic microcentrifuge rack (shielded from light) 00:01:00 n.b. It can be helpful to partially open the tubes before placing them in the magnetic rack so that they can be opened all the way after incubation without disturbing the beads more than necessary
Formulate protein for coupling @ approx. 25 µg/mL in 1x PBS 250 µL

Wash by removing 90 µL of supernatant and replacing with 100 µL of 1x PBS; pulse vortex to mix and resuspend beads This introduces a 10 µL excess that will protect against bead aspiration throughout the remainder of the protocol where equal volumes will be removed and added
Incubate with end-over-end mixing (shielded from light) 00:20:00 Room temperature

Remove supernatant and resuspend in a total of 90 µL consisting of: - sulfo-NHS 12.5 mg/mL made in 10 millimolar (mM) MES (pH 5.0) 5 µL - EDC 12.5 mg/mL , made in 10 millimolar (mM) MES (pH 5.0) 5 µL - 10 millimolar (mM) MES (pH 5.0) 80 µL
go to step # to separate beads from the supernatant
go to step # to separate the beads from the supernatant
go to step # to separate the beads from the supernatant
Remove supernatant and resuspend in protein of interest ( 250 µL )
Incubate with end-over-end mixing (shielded from light) 02:00:00 Room temperature

Remove supernatant and replace with PBS-TBN 400 µL n.b. Whenever volume of supernatant exceeds 200 µL, use a P200 pipette multiple times - the opening of a P1000 will aspirate too many beads PBS-TBN Teknova Catalog #P0210
go to step # for a total of 3 washes
Remove supernatant and replace with PBS-TBN 250 µL PBS-TBN Teknova Catalog #P0210
Block via 16:00:00, (ie. overnight) 4 °C
Will the beads be used for an assay on the same day as production?
Block via 00:30:00 Room temperature
Will the beads be used for an assay on the same day as production?
Block via 00:30:00 Room temperature
Make serial dilutions of 1:100, 1:1000, and 1:10,000

Measure concentration by flow cytometry using the fluorescent dye of the microsphere as the trigger condition Typical yield is approx. 1 - 2 x 10 8 beads, or around 25% of the original amount MACS Quant (flow cytometer, Miltenyi, 130-096-343)
In a non-binding, 96-well plate, formulate serial dilutions of positive and negative control antibodies beginning at 5 µg/mL and diluted by 1:3 over an approx. 4 point series
Add 100,000 beads to each well
Incubate with shaking (shielded from light) 01:00:00 Room temperature
Pellet by centrifugation 00:05:00 and remove supernatant by decanting
Resuspend beads in PBST (ie. 1x PBS + 0.05 % volume Tween20) 200 µL
go to step #24 for a total of 2 washes

Resuspend beads in fluorescent secondary 100 µL 0.65 µg/mL n.b. Make sure that the fluorescent molecule chosen for the secondary does not have spectral overlap with the dye in the microspheres themselves
Incubate with shaking (shielded from light) 00:30:00 Room temperature
Pellet by centrifugation 00:05:00 and remove supernatant by decanting
Resuspend beads in PBST (ie. 1x PBS + 0.05 % volume Tween20) 200 µL
Pellet by centrifugation 00:05:00 and remove supernatant by decanting
Resuspend beads in 1x PBS 100 µL
Measure fluorescent intensity by flow cytometry MACS Quant (flow cytometer, Miltenyi, 130-096-343)
creator: Andrew Crowley
